title: CLAUDE.md
text: shiny.element R package wraps Element-UI (Vue 2) components Shiny widgets, using vueR bridge Vue htmlwidgets.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Every Vue-backed component follows structure: R function (R/el_*.R) — builds HTML tags, creates Vue instance via vueR::vue(), attaches JS handler dependency. JS handler (inst/js/el-*-handler.js) — registers Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler receive server-side update messages. Update function (update_el_*) — R file, sends session$sendCustomMessage(...) corresponding JS handler. Vue instance mounts <div id="{ns_id}_container">, vueR::vue(elementId = ns_id, ...) holds Vue data methods. Component IDs always namespaced via session$ns(id) support Shiny modules.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: el_page() — top-level page wrapper; loads Vue, Element-UI CDN, vue-handlers.js, layout CSS. Wraps shiny::fluidPage() bslib Bootstrap 5. use_element() — alternative non-el_page contexts (bslib, navbarPage). Place top UI. component also calls attachDependencies() handler JS, components work even without use_element() / el_page().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: inst/js/vue-handlers.js (loaded use_element()) registers two universal handlers R/vue_update.R: update_vue_component(session, id, ...) — updates named fields Vue instance’s $data. update_vue_data(session, id, data) — replaces entire $data Vue instance. Prefer components yet covered dedicated update_el_* function.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/el_tags.R exports el — named list tag-generator functions covering Element-UI components (e.g. el$table(...), el$steps(...)). produce plain HTML tags Vue instance Shiny binding. Use static markup composing components manually.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: template(..., slot, scope) R/template.R generates Vue <template> tag slot usage (e.g. slot="dateCell" slot-scope). Returns htmltools::HTML.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Create R/el_<name>.R widget function, update_el_<name>() function, private el_<name>_handler_dependency(). Create inst/js/el-<name>-handler.js Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler('updateEl<Name>', ...). Add handler dependency function R/el_dependencies.R. Document roxygen2 run devtools::document().

text: Tests use mock_session list avoid live Shiny session: Test HTML output converting string: paste(.character(tag), collapse = "").

what: Using bslib Layouts with Element-UI Components
title: Element UI (Vue2) Integration Helpers for Shiny via vueR
text: can use Element-UI components (el_button) inside bslib layouts. Just call use_element() top UI load required dependencies, use Element-UI widgets inside bslib layout functions: Note: Element-UI layout functions (el_container, el_row, el_col) experimental fundamentally different bslib’s layout system (page_sidebar, layout_columns). directly nested mixed. use cases, prefer bslib layouts Element-UI components. Element-UI layout functions may deprecated future releases.
code: library(shiny) library(shiny.element) library(bslib)  cascader_options <- list(   list(     value = "guide",     label = "Guide",     children = list(       list(value = "principle", label = "Principle"),       list(value = "navigation", label = "Navigation")     )   ),   list(     value = "component",     label = "Component",     children = list(       list(value = "basic", label = "Basic"),       list(value = "form", label = "Form")     )   ) )  ui <- page_sidebar(   use_element(), # Loads Element-UI dependencies   sidebar = sidebar(     el_button("sidebar_btn", "Sidebar Button", type = "primary")   ),   layout_columns(     col_widths = c(4, 4, 12),     row_heights = c(2, 8),     el_button("btn1", "Primary", type = "primary"),     el_cascader(       id = "cascader1",       options = cascader_options,       placeholder = "Please select",       clearable = TRUE     ),     el_table(       id = "table1",       data = list(         list(RowName = "A", Value = 1),         list(RowName = "B", Value = 2)       ),       columns = list(         list(prop = "RowName", label = "Row Name", width = "120"),         list(prop = "Value", label = "Value", width = "80")       )     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$button1, {     showNotification(paste("Button clicked!", "Count:", input$button1))   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)
